checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document. No fabricated sections, sentences, or paragraphs were identified. The headings, table content, and committee meeting lists all correspond to information explicitly present in the PDF.
The meeting location address (3514 Bush Street, Raleigh, NC) in the output matches the source document exactly. No additional phone numbers, emails, or URLs were hallucinated.

All sections from both pages of the source PDF are present in the JSON. The agenda items 1-16, the committee meeting schedule, and the upcoming board meeting notice are all captured. The table spanning two pages has been correctly reconstructed into a single logical table.

The source document's main table contains approximately 18 logical data rows (Call to Order, Roll Call, items 1-16). The output JSON table contains exactly 18 data rows, successfully merging content from page 1 and page 2.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output preserves substantial details from the source, specifically the multi-line presenter lists and the complex financial figures (Balance and Disbursement amounts) listed under the Consent Agenda. No major omissions were noted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Text content, names, and numbers match the source document accurately. OCR for the financial figures is precise (e.g., $44,749,222). Reformatting of multi-line cells into single strings with newline characters is an acceptable structural choice.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly extracts the text content once. While the logo image appears twice, this accurately reflects the source PDF which has the logo on both page 1 and page 2. No paragraphs, list items, or table cells are duplicated.

The output uses Section titles ('AGENDA', 'AUGUST 2019 COMMITTEE MEETINGS', 'AUGUST 2019 BOARD MEETING') to organize the document. Inside the first section, it uses a Level 2 heading for the main board meeting title. This represents a logical, non-skipping hierarchy that correctly prioritizes the document's main divisions.

The main agenda is correctly identified as a table with three columns (Tab, Topic, Presenter). The pipeline successfully merged the agenda items from Page 1 (items 1-8) and Page 2 (items 9-16) into a single logical table structure, which is a structural optimization. Headers are correctly identified, and row/column alignment matches the visual intent of the source.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the agenda: header information -> main agenda table -> future committee meetings -> future board meeting notice. This aligns perfectly with the intended sequence of the source document.

The list of August committee meetings on page 2, which uses decorative glyphs as bullets in the source, is correctly converted into a standard unordered list in the JSON. The nesting and itemization are accurate.
The source document contains no hyperlinks, and the output correctly reflects this by not injecting any fabricated links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output collapses structured lists and associated data into single table cells, significantly altering the visual organization compared to the source PDF. In item 5, the nested list structure and right-aligned financial columns are flattened into a single block of text within one cell, making the budget data difficult to parse. In item 6, sub-items (a-e) are flattened into one text block, and the presenters (Pokey Harris and Richard Bradford) are combined into a single cell; this destroys the visual mapping in the original document where Richard Bradford is specifically aligned with the 'Legislative Update' sub-item. Additionally, the 'Adjourn' text, which is clearly outside the table on page 2 of the PDF, has been merged into the 'Other' row of the HTML table.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 6 'Executive Director Report' includes sub-items a-e with 'Pokey Harris (05)' aligned to the top and 'Richard Bradford' aligned specifically with sub-item 'e'. Item 5 'Consent Agenda' shows a structured sub-list with financial figures aligned in a right-hand column.
checks.b_evidence_output: Row 6 in the HTML table merges all sub-items into one cell and both presenters into another. Row 5 topic cell contains a run-on string of text: 'Consent Agenda (Vote Required) a) Minutes of Previous Meeting – June 28, 2019 b) NG 911 Fund June 2019 Fund Balance $ 44,749,222...'
checks.b_evidence_location: Table rows 5, 6, and 16, corresponding to pages 1 and 2 of the PDF

checks.a_analysis: The main agenda table is rendered correctly as a table with consistent columns (Tab, Topic, Presenter). Borders are visible, and headers are visually distinct from the body rows due to the background shading. While the content within some cells is poorly formatted (addressed in V1), the table structure itself is not broken or corrupted.

The meaningful NC DIT 911 Board logo from the header of the source document is present in the HTML output. While the styling has changed (black background in HTML vs white in PDF), the icon and text elements are preserved and visible.
A thorough review of the HTML output shows no instances of duplicated body content. Each agenda item and section appears only once in the main content area.
